# ADR-001: Foo choice

## Context

The crate needed a strategy for handling the Foo dimension of its token
workflow. Two candidates were on the table: option X (a self-contained
implementation) and option Y (a thin wrapper around an external library).

## Decision

We chose X over Y because X offers better long-term control over the
verification path and avoids a runtime dependency that would bloat the
fixture crate used by downstream tests.

## Consequences

We accept a small amount of extra maintenance in exchange for a tighter
public API and faster compile times.
